title: arundarbor maxima
side_effects: Arundarbor maxima can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins. In some cases, excessive consumption can also lead to allergic reactions, manifesting as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. Furthermore, the plant's toxic compounds can cause liver damage and kidney problems if ingested in large amounts. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can also cause skin irritation and burns, particularly if it comes into contact with open wounds.
